description: Configuration for adding measurement noise to features to simulate data quality heterogeneity across clients

Periodontal_Status.description: Periodontal status assessment can vary between clinicians

Tooth_Mobility.description: Mobility assessment is subjective and varies significantly

Tooth_Angulation.description: Angle classification can vary based on imaging and interpretation

Proximity_Nerve.description: Imaging interpretation varies, especially for borderline cases

Trismus.description: Measurement of mouth opening can vary

Smoking.description: Patient history is usually accurate, very low noise

noise_model.description: How noise is applied to features

ordinal_shift.description: Ordinal features shift by ±1 with probability (noise_level * noise_multiplier)
ordinal_shift.clipping: Values are clipped to [min, max] range after shift

binary_flip.description: Binary features flip (0-1) with probability (noise_level * noise_multiplier)

usage.description: Each client has a base measurement_noise level (e.g., 0.025). The actual probability of noise for a feature is: base_noise * noise_multiplier
usage.example: Client with measurement_noise=0.05 will have 0.05*1.5=0.075 (7.5%) chance of Periodontal_Status shifting by ±1
